raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.3,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The contribution claim and gap statement are present but not maximally sharp; 'stronger' plausibly requires tightening the thesis framing (what exactly TermBridge uniquely solves and why prior constrained-decoding approaches are structurally insufficient).",
    "R1.2": "The causal chain from training-procedure design to the observed gains has gaps (e.g., no ablation cited for the inline-vs-planning-pass design choice, no confidence intervals on human preference); 'stronger' plausibly means closing these logical holes.",
    "R1.6": "'Stronger' very commonly denotes prose-level impact — sharper topic sentences, tighter transitions, more forceful framing of the numbers — making writing polish a central candidate responsibility."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Does 'stronger' mean primarily improving sentence-level rhetoric and prose impact (R1.6 load-bearing), or primarily sharpening the scientific argument — tightening the contribution claim, closing logical gaps in the method-to-result chain, and hardening the comparison to prior work (R1.1/R1.2 load-bearing)?"
}

rationale.R1.1: The contribution claim and gap statement are present but not maximally sharp; 'stronger' plausibly requires tightening the thesis framing (what exactly TermBridge uniquely solves and why prior constrained-decoding approaches are structurally insufficient).
rationale.R1.2: The causal chain from training-procedure design to the observed gains has gaps (e.g., no ablation cited for the inline-vs-planning-pass design choice, no confidence intervals on human preference); 'stronger' plausibly means closing these logical holes.
rationale.R1.6: 'Stronger' very commonly denotes prose-level impact — sharper topic sentences, tighter transitions, more forceful framing of the numbers — making writing polish a central candidate responsibility.

parsed.clarification_question: Does 'stronger' mean primarily improving sentence-level rhetoric and prose impact (R1.6 load-bearing), or primarily sharpening the scientific argument — tightening the contribution claim, closing logical gaps in the method-to-result chain, and hardening the comparison to prior work (R1.1/R1.2 load-bearing)?
